perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a commission (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Bonus will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'royalty' is initially defined but then 'Bonus' is introduced as the thing that 'will cease being paid upon the death of Individual', creating confusion as to what the payment is. This contradicts itself, when saying that the Royalty will not exceed $50,000, creating uncertainty in financial obligations and introducing a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a commission (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Bonus will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the term 'Bonus' as the item that ceases upon death, while the section also refers to 'Royalty' and states the 'Royalty' will not exceed $50,000. The contradicted text also refers to both 'Bonus' and 'Royalty', creating uncertainty as to which is the correct payment method and how payments should be processed. This creates a contradiction regarding what payment is being referred to in the sentence, making the financial obligations unclear and contradicting the definition of misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings.
perturbation.changed_text: License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, fee-based, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "royalty-free" to "fee-based", the contract now contradicts section 9, where a specific royalty payment is defined. This creates uncertainty as to whether the usage rights are royalty-free or require payment of the defined royalty, even though it states on the contrary on Section 9
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.

perturbation.original_text: Confidentiality - With respect to this Agreement and any information supplied in connection with this Agreement and designated by the disclosing party as confidential, the recipient agrees to: (i) protect the confidential information in a reasonable and appropriate manner; (ii) use confidential information only to perform its obligations under this Agreement; and (iii) reproduce confidential information only as required to perform its obligations under this Agreement. This section shall not apply to information that is: (i) publicly known; (ii) already known to the recipient; (iii) disclosed to a third party without restriction; (iv) independently developed; or (v) disclosed pursuant to legal requirement or order.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidentiality - With respect to this Agreement and any information supplied in connection with this Agreement and designated by the disclosing party as proprietary, the recipient agrees to: (i) protect the confidential information in a reasonable and appropriate manner; (ii) use confidential information only to perform its obligations under this Agreement; and (iii) reproduce confidential information only as required to perform its obligations under this Agreement. This section shall not apply to information that is: (i) publicly known; (ii) already known to the recipient; (iii) disclosed to a third party without restriction; (iv) independently developed; or (v) disclosed pursuant to legal requirement or order.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of the term 'proprietary' alongside 'confidential' without defining whether they are interchangeable creates ambiguity. While the section header is 'Confidentiality', the modified text uses 'proprietary' in the definition, leading to potential disputes about the scope of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality - With respect to this Agreement and any information supplied in connection with this Agreement and designated by the disclosing party as proprietary, the recipient agrees to: (i) protect the confidential information in a reasonable and appropriate manner; (ii) use confidential information only to perform its obligations under this Agreement; and (iii) reproduce confidential information only as required to perform its obligations under this Agreement. This section shall not apply to information that is: (i) publicly known; (ii) already known to the recipient; (iii) disclosed to a third party without restriction; (iv) independently developed; or (v) disclosed pursuant to legal requirement or order.
perturbation.justification: The provided changed text and contradicted text are identical. While the explanation suggests the introduction of 'proprietary' alongside 'confidential' without definition could create ambiguity, the identical nature of the sections means there is no in-text contradiction. If the term was explicitly defined in another place as separate from 'confidential', and the text then required the term, this could cause a contradiction. As is, it is just a possible ambiguity.
